Database Management System (CSGE201) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objectives 
The course introduces the concepts of database management systems to students, focusing on 
basics such as the importance and significance of a database, data model, schema creation 
and normalization. 
 
Course Learning Outcomes

On successful completion of the course, students will be able to: 
1. Describe the features of database management systems. 
2. Differentiate between database systems and file systems. 
3. Model an application’s data requirements using conceptual modelling tools like ER 
diagrams and design database schemas based on the conceptual model. 
4. Write queries in relational algebra / SQL. 
5. Normalize a given database schema. 
 
Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1 
Introduction: Introduction to database, relational data model, DBMS architecture, data 
independence and data abstraction, DBA, database users, end users, front end tools 
Unit 2 
Data Modelling: Entity types, entity set, attribute and key, relationships, relation types, ER 
diagrams, database design using ER diagrams 
Unit 3 
Relational Data Model: Relational model concepts, relational constraints, primary and 
foreign key, candidate key, alternate, composite, super-key. 
Unit 4 
Data redundancy, Normalization: 1NF, 2NF, 3NF. 
Unit 5 
Structured Query Language: Introduction to SQL, concepts of Data Definition Language 
(DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML), DDL queries like create a data base, drop a 
database, create table, drop table, alter table, DML queries like inserting data in a table, 
update in a table, delete data from a table, filter data. 
Unit 6 
Structured Query Language (continued..): Create relationships between database tables, 
auto increment, check, Null values, aggregate functions - min, max, count, average, sum, 
nested sub-queries, group by, having, exists, case, order by. Join operations - inner, left join, 
right join, natural join and Cartesian product. Overview of forms and reports. 
 Practicals: 
1. Create a database having two tables with the specified fields, to computerize a library 
system of a Delhi University College. 
Library Books (Accession number, Title, Author, Department, Purchase Date, Price)

Issued Books (Accession number, Borrower) 
 
a) Identify primary and foreign keys. Create the tables and insert at least 5 records in 
each table. 
b) Delete the record of book titled “Database System Concepts”. 
c) Change the Department of the book titled “Discrete Maths” to “CS”. 
d) List all books that belong to “CS” department. 
e) List all books that belong to “CS” department and are written by author “Navathe”. 
f) List all computer (Department = “CS”) that have been issued. 
g) List all books which have a price less than 500 or purchased between “01/01/1999” 
and “01/01/2004”. 
2. Create a database having three tables to store the details of students of Computer 
Department in your college, as per the given schema.

Address, Marks(rounded off to whole number) in percentage at 10 + 2, Phone number) 
Paper Details (Paper code, Name of the Paper) 
Student’s Academic and Attendance details (College roll number, Paper code, 
Attendance, Marks in home examination). 
 
a) Identify primary and foreign keys. Create the tables and insert at least 5 records in 
each table. 
b) Design a query that will return the records (from the second table) along with the 
name of student from the first table, related to students who have more than 75% 
attendance and more than 60% marks in paper 2. 
c) List all students who live in “Delhi” and have marks greater than 60 in paper 1. 
d) Find the total attendance and total marks obtained by each student. 
e) List the name of student who has got the highest marks in paper 2. 
3. Create the following tables and answer the queries given below:

a) Identify primary and foreign keys. Create the tables and insert at least 5 records in 
each table. 
b) List all the customers who have the bicycles manufactured by manufacturer 
“Honda”. 
c) List the bicycles purchased by the customers who have been referred by customer 
“C1”. 
d) List the manufacturer of red colored bicycles. 
e) List the models of the bicycles given for service. 
4. Create the following tables, enter at least 5 records in each table and answer the queries 
given below. 
EMPLOYEE ( Person_Name, Street, City )

WORKS ( Person_Name, Company_Name, Salary ) 
COMPANY ( Company_Name, City )

a) Identify primary and foreign keys.

f) Find the highest salary, lowest salary and average salary paid by each company. 
g) Find the sum of salary and number of employees in each company.h) Find the

5. Create the following tables, enter at least 5 records in each table and answer the queries 
given below. 
Suppliers (SNo, Sname, Status, SCity) 
Parts (PNo, Pname, Colour, Weight, City) 
Project (JNo, Jname, Jcity) 
Shipment (Sno, Pno, Jno, Qunatity)

b) Get supplier numbers for suppliers in Paris with status>20.

d) Get suppliers names for suppliers who do not supply part P2.

Computer Networks (CSGE301) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objective 
This course covers the fundamental concepts of computer networks, standard models of 
computer networks having layered architecture, data communication, network topologies, 
web technologies and internet applications. 
Course Learning Outcomes 
On successful completion of this course, a student will be able to: 
1. State the use of computer networks and different network topologies. 
2. Distinguish between LAN, MAN, WAN, and between Intranet, Extranet and Internet. 
3. Compare OSI and TCP/IP architectures 
4. Enumerate different transmission media and describe the use of each of them. 
5. Design web pages using HTML. 
 
Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1

Computer Networks: Introduction to computer network, data communication, components 
of data communication, data transmission mode, data communication measurement, LAN, 
MAN, WAN, wireless LAN, internet, intranet, extranet. 
Unit II 
Network Models: Client/ server network and Peer-to-peer network, OSI, TCP/IP, layers and 
functionalities. 
Unit III 
Transmission Media: Introduction, Guided Media: Twisted pair, Coaxial cable, Optical 
fiber. Unguided media: Microwave, Radio frequency propagation, Satellite. 
Unit IV 
LAN Topologies: Ring, bus, star, mesh and tree topologies. Network Devices: NIC, 
repeaters, hub, bridge, switch, gateway and router. 
Unit V 
Internet Terms: Web page, Home page, website, internet browsers, URL, Hypertext, ISP, 
Web server, download and upload, online and offline. Internet Applications: www, telnet, ftp, 
e-mail, social networks, search engines, Video Conferencing, e-Commerce, m-Commerce, 
VOIP, blogs. 
Unit VI 
Introduction to Web Design: Introduction to hypertext mark-up language (html), Document 
type definition, creating web pages, lists, hyperlinks, tables, web forms, inserting images, 
frames, hosting options and domain name registration. Customized Features: Cascading style 
sheet (CSS) for text formatting and other manipulations. 
Practical 
Networking exercises in a trial lab, where effects of different connectors, topologies in 
practical could be demonstrated. 
 
Before moving to Java Script practicals, students must get an idea about fundamental 
programing using Scratch (https://scratch.mit.edu). Students should be encouraged to learn 
Scratch 
on 
their 
own 
and 
work 
on 
assignments 
available 
online 
such 
as 
https://sites.google.com/site/christopherscfahs/scratch-programming/scratch-assignments 
or 
http://scratched.gse.harvard.edu/resources/uw-catapult-project. 
 
Alternatively, students may use Alice (http://www.alice.org/index.php) and learn basic 
programming. A lot of online assignments and spoken tutorials on You Tube would be 
helpful.

Information Security and Cyber Laws (CSGE401) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objectives 
This course introduces the students to the concepts of information security and different type 
of attacks in the cyber space. The course also introduces countermeasures to mitigate attacks 
and different existing cyber laws. 
 
Course Learning Outcomes 
On successful completion of the course, students will be able to: 
1. Learn, structure, mechanics and evolution of various crime threats 
2. Learn to protect information systems from external attacks by developing skills in 
enterprise security, wireless security and computer forensics. 
3. Analyse the risks involved while sharing their information in cyber space and 
numerous related solutions like sending protected and digitally signed documents 
4. Insights of ethical hacking and usage of password cracking tools 
5. Get an overview of different ciphers used for encryption and decryption. 
 
Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1 
Definitions :Protection, Security, risk, threat, vulnerability, exploit, attack, confidentiality, 
integrity, availability, non-repudiation, authentication , authorization, codes, plain text, 
encryption, 
decryption, 
cipher 
text, 
key, 
ciphers, 
Symmetric 
and 
asymmetric

cryptography, Public key , private key ,Crypt analysis,, Cyber forensics. Substitution cipher 
(Caesar), Transposition cipher (Rail-Fence), 
Unit 2 
Risk analysis, process, key principles of conventional computer security, security policies, 
data protection, access control, internal vs external threat, security assurance, 
passwords, access control, computer forensics and incident response. 
Unit 3 
CYBER ATTACKS (definitions and examples): Denial-of-service attacks, Man-in-themiddle attack, Phishing, spoofing and spam attacks, Drive-by attack, Password attack, SQL 
injection attack, Cross-site scripting attack, Eavesdropping attack, Birthday attack, Malware 
attacks, Social Engineering attacks 
Unit 4 
Brief Introduction of handling the attacks described in UNIT 3. Firewalls, logging and 
intrusion detection systems, e-mail security, security issues in operating systems, ethics of 
hacking and cracking. 
Unit 5 
Definitions: Digital Signature and Electronic Signature, Digital Certificate 
i. [Section 43] Penalty and compensation for damage to computer etc. 
ii. [Section 65] Tampering with computer source documents 
iii. [Section 66A] Punishment for sending offensive messages through communication 
service etc. 
iv. [Section 66B] Punishment for dishonestly receiving stolen computer resource or 
communication device 
v. [Section 66C] Punishment for identity theft 
vi. [Section 66D] Punishment for cheating by impersonation by using computer resource 
vii. [Section 66E] Punishment for violation of privacy 
viii. [Section 66F] Punishment for cyber terrorism 
ix. [Section 67] Punishment for publishing or transmitting obscene material in electronic 
form 
x. [Section 67A] Punishment for publishing or transmitting of material 
containing sexually explicit act, etc. in electronic form 
xi. [Section 67B] Punishment for publishing or transmitting of material depicting 
children in sexually explicit act, etc. in electronic form 
xii. [Section 72] Breach of confidentiality and privacy 
 
Unit 6 
Brief introduction of IT infrastructure in India, National agencies handling IT.

Practical 
 
1. Demonstrate the use of Network tools: ping, ipconfig, ifconfig, tracert, arp, netstat, 
whois 
2. Use of Password cracking tools : John the Ripper, Ophcrack. Verify the strength of 
passwords using these tools. 
3. Perform encryption and decryption of Caesar cipher. Write a script for performing 
these operations. 
4. Perform encryption and decryption of a Rail fence cipher. Write a script for 
performing these operations. 
5. Use nmap/zenmap to analyse a remote machine. 
6. Use Burp proxy to capture and modify the message. 
7. Demonstrate sending of a protected word document. 
8. Demonstrate sending of a digitally signed document. 
9. Demonstrate sending of a protected worksheet. 
10. Demonstrate use of steganography tools. 
11. Demonstrate use of gpg utility for signing and encrypting purposes. 
 
References

edition. Prentice Hall. 
2. Snyder, G.F. (2010). Network Security, Cengage Learning. 
3. Whitman, M. E. & Mattord, H. J. (2017) Principles of Information Security. 6th 
edition. Cengage Learning.

IT Fundamentals (CSGE501) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objectives 
This course introduces the students to the basic concepts of computers. The aim is to bridge 
the fundamental concepts of computers with the present level of knowledge of the students. 
The course also aims to skill the students so that they can make use of information 
technology effectively in all walks of life. 
 
Course Learning Outcomes 
On successful completion of this course, students will be able to: 
1. develop a vocabulary of key terms related to the computer and to software program 
menus, identify the components of a personal computer system and use the interface 
deftly. 
2. organize files and documents on storage devices. 
3. compose, format and edit a word document. 
4. use spreadsheet for storing data and performing preliminary analysis. 
5. acquire fundamental knowledge of networking and distinguish between different 
types of networks. 
6. acquire knowledge of internet applications and use them. 
 
Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1 
Introduction: Introduction to logical organization of computer, input and output devices, 
keyboard, mouse, joystick, scanner, OCR, OMR, monitor, Printer, Plotter. 
Unit 2 
Storage Devices: Primary memory, secondary memory, auxiliary memory. 
Unit 3

Multimedia and Web Design (CSGE601) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objective 
This paper aims to provide comprehensive review of concepts and techniques for designing 
and developing attractive websites with multimedia components. 
 
Course Learning Outcomes 
On successful completion of the course, students will be able to: 
● understand fundamental Web design principles and technologies· 
● understand the detailed design plan required to create a successful Web site that consi
ders audience needs, accessibility features, and various technical issues. 
● understand the coverage of ownership, permissions, and copyright issues· 
● incorporate text, images, animation, sound, and video into Web pages. 
● create a website with popular multimedia authoring tools, such as Macromedia Flash. 
 
Detailed Syllabus: 
Unit 1 
Introduction: Definition of multimedia, Components, uses, applications 
Unit 2

Multimedia Input/ Output Devices: Scanner, camera, microphone, speaker, monitors, 
printers 
Unit 3 
Multimedia Storage Devices: CD ROMs, DVDs, Blue ray disk 
Unit 4 
Multimedia tools: Sound editor, video editor, animator, authoring tools 
Unit 5 
Web Designing: Concept of website, website as a communication resource, Internet, intranet 
and extranet, basic concepts related to website designing. 
Unit 6 
HTML: Introduction to hypertext markup language, document type definition, creating web 
pages, graphical elements, lists, hyperlinks, tables, web forms, inserting images, inserting 
audio, video and animation; frames, use of CSS. 
 
Practical

automation tool/ GIMP/Blender / Audacity/ Animation Tools/ Image Editors/ Video Editors.

Data Science using R (CSGE602) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objective 
Data Science also known as data driven science makes use of scientific methods, processes 
and systems to extract knowledge or insights from data in various forms (structured or 
unstructured). The study of these methods and processes is the main objective of the course. 
Course Learning Outcomes 
On successful completion of the course, students will be able to: 
1. create effective solutions to data analysis challenges. 
2. effectively organize and manage data science projects. 
3. analyse problems and identify solutions. 
4. communicate data science problems, methods, and findings effectively, visually, and 
in writing.

Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1 
Introduction: Introduction to Data Science, Data Science Basics, Need of Data Science, Life 
Cycle of Data Science, Challenges of Data Science Technology, Data Science Components, 
,Applications of Data Science, Data Science Job Roles, Tools for Data Science, Structured 
Data, Unstructured Data. 
Unit 2 
R Programming Basics: Overview of R, R data types and objects, Operators, Precedence 
and associativity, Decision Making, Loops, Functions, Scoping, File System, Reading and 
writing Data 
Unit 3 
Data Structures In R programming: Strings, Vectors, Lists, Matrices, Arrays, 
Factors, Data Frames. 
 
Unit 4 
Statistical Analysis: Mean, Harmonic Mean, Median, Mode, Standard Deviation, Central 
tendency, measures of dispersion, Data Distribution, Analysis of Variance & covariance, 
Summary Function, Z score Function, Linear Regression analysis, Multiple Regression, 
Logistic regression. 
Unit 5 
Data Visualization: Overview of Data Visualization, Pie Chart, Line Graphs, Bar Chart, 
Histogram, Box plot, Scatter Plot, Function plot, Animating objects using R. 
Unit 6 
Case Study: Suitable Case Studies will be taken up. The list will be provided by the 
department in consultation with the college teachers in the beginning of the semester. 
 
Practical 
 
Q1. Write an R script to do the following: 
a) Draw 10 random letters from the lowercase alphabet, and sort them alphabetically. 
b) Draw 5 random letters from each of the lowercase and uppercase alphabets, 
incorporating both into a single vector, and sort it alphabetically. 
c) Repeat the above exercise but sort the vector alphabetically in descending order.

Q2. Read a text file that contains sales of milk in litres for 15 days in three different shops (the 
first 3 values are for shops 1,2 and 3 on Monday, etc.). Produce a statistical summary of 
the sales for each day of the week and also for each shop. 
 
Q3. Read the rainfall in cm of three different states S1, S2 and S3 from a CSV file. Write an R 
script to plot an appropriate graph to compare them using different colours. Label the 
graph, x-axis, y-axis and the legend. 
 
Q4. Read a text file containing the monthly expenditure breakdown of an individual as 
{Housing, Food, Clothing, entertainment, Others}. 
 Write an R script to: 
a) represent monthly expenditure breakdown using a pie chart. 
b) represent the percentage of each expenditure using pie chart. Also, add chart legend to 
the graph. 
Q5. Write an R script to do the following: 
a) Simulate a sample of 100 random data points from a normal distribution with mean 100 
and standard deviation 5, and store the result in a vector. 
b) Plot a histogram and a boxplot of the vector you just created. 
c) Calculate the summary of the sample generated. 
d) Using the data above, test the hypothesis that the mean equals 100. 
e) Test the hypothesis that mean equals 90. 
 
 
Use data set of your choice from Open Data Portal (https://data.gov.in/) for the following 
exercises. 
Q6. Read the all rows from the CSV file, with header. Write an R script to: 
a) create a subset of the data records that satisfy a condition. 
b) find suitable descriptive statistics for each column. 
c) draw boxplot for the numeric attributes and identify outliers, if any 
d) find correlation for each pair of numeric attributes, and draw scatter plot matrix. 
e) draw histograms, pie charts for categorical attributes. 
f) draw the probability density curve for numeric attributes. 
Projects and Case Studies to be done as decided by the department in the beginning of the 
semester. 
References 
1. Wickham, H. R. & Grolemund, G. (2017). R for Data Science. 1st edition. O'Reilly Media.

Additional Resources

Learning. Springer. 
2. Rahif, T. (2017). Data Visualization with R. Springer.

Data Science using Python (CSGE603) Generic Elective - (GE) 
Credit: 06 
 
Course Objective 
Data Science also known as data driven science makes use of scientific methods, processes 
and systems to extract knowledge or insights from structured and unstructured data. This 
paper is designed to introduce the methods and processes of Data Science. 
Course Learning Outcomes 
After completion of the course, students will be able to: 
1. analyse and solve data science problems in real world projects. 
2. effectively organize and manage data sets for data science projects. 
3. use Python packages to perform statistical analysis of given dataset. 
4. display data in suitable visual form. 
 
Detailed Syllabus 
Unit 1 
Introduction: Introduction to Data Science, Data Science Basics, Need of Data Science, Life 
Cycle of Data Science, Challenges of Data Science Technology, Data Science Components, 
Applications of Data Science, Data Science Job Roles, Tools for Data Science, Structured 
Data, Unstructured Data.

Over the past three decades, Maharaja Agrasen College, University of Delhi has distinguished itself 
as one of the premier academic institutions in the country. With state-of-the-art infrastructure and 
vibrant intellectual spaces, the college vitalises critical thinking, numerous participatory discussions 
and debates, and a holistic development and an inclusive environment. Its devoted faculty, acclaimed 
for academic rigour and professional integrity, its efficient administrative support, and its verdant, 
nurturing campus environment together create a space that inspires both cognitive growth and ethical 
and creative stimulation.
The college boasts advanced learning facilities including smart classrooms, a modern auditorium, 
ICT-enabled rooms, a conference hall, and a well-stocked library equipped with electronic resources. 
Students benefit from amenities such as a gymnasium, meticulously maintained sports grounds, and 
Vidyottama, the residential accommodation for female students. A noteworthy recent addition is 
Sahastradhara, an open micro-learning platform designed to enhance creativity and innovation.
A wide spectrum of co-curricular committees, clubs, centres, and departmental initiatives manifest 
academic engagement with dynamic skill-building. These forums cultivate leadership, teamwork, 
and organisational, and collaborative skills, offering students to widen the democratic avenues for 
intellectual and creative expression, and empower them with new perspectives and bold ideas.
The college's Environment Cell, through its dynamic and proactive sustainability efforts, has 
earned MAC the distinction of being a zero-waste campus. Whether through its green ethos or its 
comprehensive support for professional and socio-ethical growth seen in a range of platforms like 
the NCC Girls Cadets, Placement Cell, Abdul Kalam Centre of Research, Centre for Civic Education, 
Centre for Performing Arts and Cultural Studies, Sanskriti Kala Kendra, and various student societies, 
MAC offers a rich, motivating ecosystem for young learners to evolve into responsible and enlightened 
citizens.
At Maharaja Agrasen College, you will find an empowering space to explore creativity, engage in 
original research, build your academic aptitude, participate in insightful dialogues on social relevance, 
and contribute significantly towards a sustainable and inclusive future.
I extend my heartfelt wishes for your continued success, growth, and openness to the many 
possibilities that await you all as you embark on this new chapter of your lives.
Prof. Rakesh Kumar
Chairman, Governing Body

Prof. Sanjeev Kumar Tiwari
Principal
It gives me immense joy to welcome all students joining Maharaja Agrasen College for the 
academic session 2025–26. I congratulate each one of you for clearing the CUET examination 
and securing a place in one of the most vibrant institutions of Delhi University. This marks the 
beginning of a journey filled with growth, discovery, and purpose.
This year is particularly significant as the fourth year of undergraduate education, under the 
framework of the National Education Policy (NEP), has been officially rolled out. This reform is 
a major stride toward promoting flexible, research-driven, and multidisciplinary education. The 
NEP also emphasizes vocational training and skill development to prepare youths for real-world 
challenges.
As Bharat emerges as the 4th largest economy in the world, the responsibility of sustaining 
and advancing this growth lies with its youth. The knowledge and skills you acquire here will 
shape not just your individual future but also the future of our nation. As Swami Vivekananda 
rightly said, "Education is the manifestation of the perfection already in man." We believe in 
nurturing that potential through value-based, inclusive, and forward-looking education.
Maharaja Agrasen College, accredited with an ‘A’ Grade, proudly ranked 23rd in Science 
stream, 26th in Arts and 35th in Commerce in the MDRA-India Today College Rankings 2025, 
continues to be a symbol of academic excellence. Our 10-acre campus houses state-of-the-art 
infrastructure which includes modern laboratories, an advanced ICT Centre, a fully equipped 
auditorium, spacious conference halls, a rich library with over 50,000 titles, and smart, interactive 
classrooms.
Our faculty has enthusiastically embraced the NEP’s vision, undertaking numerous research 
projects and contributing to an intellectually stimulating environment. Dedicated centres such 
as the Abdul Kalam Centre, Centre for Performing Arts and Culture, Sanskriti Kala Kendra, and 
Civic Education Centre further encourage student-led innovation and inquiry.
Beyond academics, our commitment to holistic development is unwavering. Through the 
NSS and the NCC-Girls Unit, students actively participate in social initiatives and national 
programs. The Extra Curricular Activities Committee, with its 14 dynamic societies, offers 
ample opportunities to cultivate artistic and cultural talents. The MAC-Wellness Centre and our 
expansive sports infrastructure support physical well-being and team spirit.
As you embark on this transformative journey, always keep in mind that you are stepping 
into a space that not only imparts knowledge but also shapes character. Make the most of it.
Wishing you a rewarding and enriching academic year ahead.
Prof. Sanjeev Kumar Tiwari
Principal

Maharaja Agrasen College (MAC), in just three decades since 
its inception, has emerged as one of India’s premier institutions 
for higher education in the Arts, Science, and Commerce 
streams. With a commitment to academic excellence and allround development, the college has consistently excelled in 
academics, sports, extracurricular achievements, and faculty 
advancement. It proudly retained its NAAC Grade ‘A’ in the 2022 
cycle, reflecting its unwavering pursuit of quality education.
In the recent national survey by the India Today Group and 
MDRA, MAC was ranked 23th in Science, 26th in Arts, and 
35th in Commerce—a testament to its growing prominence. 
Furthermore, its inclusion among the top 50 institutions in 
the National Institutional Ranking Framework (2022) by the 
Ministry of Education stands as a marker of its excellence. This 
success is driven by a dedicated faculty that integrates cuttingedge research and innovative pedagogies to nurture young minds.
The college remains at the forefront of educational innovation, constantly adapting to the latest technologies and teaching 
methodologies to foster a holistic learning environment. Even during the global pandemic, MAC rose to the challenge by launching 
a dedicated online learning portal and organizing a series of faculty development programmes, seminars, and workshops. The 
reopening of the University post-pandemic witnessed a strengthened resolve, with the MAC community seamlessly integrating 
acquired digital proficiencies into everyday pedagogy.
MAC’s infrastructure supports its vision: smart classrooms equipped with NET-enabled interactive panels, spacious 
auditoriums and conference halls, ICT-enabled laboratories, a well-stocked digital library, a fully functional gymnasium, an oncampus girls’ hostel that ensures a secure residential experience for outstation students, an experiential space, Sahastradhara where 
real-life learning and theoretical knowledge intersects.
Several specialized centres within the college—including the Media Centre, Centre for Civic Education, Centre for Performing 
Arts and Cultural Studies, and Sanskriti Kala Kendra—contribute to students’ holistic growth. The Abdul Kalam Centre for 
Research and the newly instituted Vivekananda Study Circle, further MAC’s efforts to embed a robust research culture among its 
students and faculty. The college is also proud to serve as a centre for IGNOU and the Non-Collegiate Women’s Education Board 
(NCWEB).
Student creativity and expression flourish through the college’s wide array of co-curricular societies and departmental 
associations. Supported by enthusiastic faculty mentors, these platforms instil values of integrity, hard work, and perseverance. The 
college remains deeply committed to inclusivity, actively supporting students from all backgrounds. Flagship student-led initiatives 
such as NSS projects—AKSHAR, SYAHI, ABHI, SAMARTHYA, and others—reflect MAC’s enduring sense of social responsibility. 
The Girls’ NCC unit, in particular, has brought honour to the college through its outstanding performance and achievements.
The college also takes pride in its vigorous promotion of sports, with many students representing the state and nation in 
diverse disciplines. Complementing its academic strength is the proactive Placement Cell, which connects students to recruiters 
and enhances their employability through regular workshops and industry interactions.
MAC’s alumni have already distinguished themselves in various professional, civic, and cultural spheres. The Maharaja Agrasen 
College Alumni Association (MACAA) fosters this growing network through annual gatherings and initiatives that maintain a 
strong bond between alumni and their alma mater.
MAC’s guiding philosophy is rooted in adaptability, resilience, and vision. With eyes set firmly on the future, it continues its 
mission to lead with excellence, embracing every challenge as an opportunity to scale new heights.
MAC PROFILE

FACILITIES
ICT Centre
The Centre supports the e-curriculum needs of computer-based practical oriented subjects across various courses offered by 
the college. In addition, it operates a dedicated general-purpose computer lab, accessible to faculty, students, and administrative 
staff for their computing requirements at their convenience. This lab offers a diverse technological environment, equipped with 
Windows 7, Mac OS, and Linux operating systems. A secure Wi-Fi network is also provided by the Centre, ensuring seamless 
internet access for all members of the college community.
Media Lab
The College houses a fully equipped Media Centre, featuring high-definition technology and in-house professional expertise to 
provide comprehensive training in both pre-production and post-production for Journalism students. In addition to academic 
instruction, the Centre serves as a hub for film screenings, short filmmaking workshops, and seminars or conferences focused on 
diverse facets of media and journalism. Hallmarked by Live Projects and active Industry Interface programmes, the Media Centre 
plays a pivotal role in bridging classroom learning with real-world media experience. The College also collaborates with industry 
partners to host various events under the aegis of the Centre.
Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel Auditorium
Housed in a dedicated three-storey complex, the state-of-the-art 
auditorium stands as a cornerstone of the College’s cultural and 
extracurricular landscape. With a seating capacity of 700, it is 
named in honour of Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, the Iron Man of 
India, and serves as the primary venue for the institution’s most 
significant academic and cultural engagements.
The auditorium has played host to a range of distinguished 
events, including the Swami Vivekanand Memorial Annual 
Lecture Series, the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel Memorial Annual 
Lecture Series, Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam Popular Science Lecture 
Series, and YUVAAN, the college’s annual cultural festival. It 
has also welcomed international artistry through SPIC MACAY 
programs, featuring performances by acclaimed groups such as Polish traditional music and dance ensembles, Indo-Georgian 
cultural exchange programmes, and the Italian band Kora Beat. 
Over the years, several eminent personalities have addressed the student community from its stage—among them, former 
Vice President of India Shri M. Venkaiah Naidu, the former Hon’ble Governor of Gujarat Shri O.P. Kohli, reverberated with the 
performances of Pt. Hariprasad Chaurasia, Kathak maestro Dr. Shovana Narayan, etc.
Conference Room
The college’s ultra-modern Conference Room is outfitted with advanced facilities, including 
a large ultra-high-resolution interactive panel, superior sound system, overhead projector, 
and other state-of-the-art technological tools. This cutting-edge infrastructure has enabled 
the seamless organization of both national and international conferences, as well as a series 
of Student Congresses and academic presentations.
 Vidyottama Girls’ Hostel
Vidyottama, the Girls’ Hostel of Maharaja Agrasen College, provides a safe, secure, and 
comfortable residential environment for female students. Spread across three floors, the hostel 
accommodates 58 residents, with designated dining facilities located on the ground floor. Each 
floor is equipped with a pantry featuring a water cooler, washing machine, microwave, induction 
stove, ironing amenities, and other essential conveniences.

FACILITIES
Admissions are granted strictly on the basis of merit, in adherence to the prevailing reservation norms. Prospective outstation 
students are advised to visit the college website—www.mac.du.ac.in—regularly for the latest updates and details regarding hostel 
admissions and related procedures.
Sports Facilities
Maharaja Agrasen College offers a comprehensive array of sports facilities, along 
with professional coaching and training in disciplines such as Athletics, Archery, 
Basketball, Badminton, Chess, Cricket, Cycling, Soccer, Korfball, Softball, Volleyball, 
and Wushu. The College Sports Committee oversees all sporting activities and 
regularly organises a wide range of sports and adventure programs for students, 
faculty, and non-teaching staff alike.
A major highlight is the annual inter-college sports festival SPARDHA, which 
fosters healthy competition and camaraderie across institutions. To further promote 
physical wellness, the College houses a fully equipped gymnasium—the MAC Wellness Centre—accessible to all students and staff 
during morning hours. For hostel residents, the facility remains available during the evenings as well, offering greater flexibility.
Looking ahead, the College envisions several new developments to expand its fitness infrastructure:
Library
At MAC, the Library serves as an intellectually vibrant hub, fostering cognitive 
growth and academic exploration. Spanning two floors, it offers a thoughtfully 
designed environment with comfortable seating for both students and faculty. 
The collection boasts over 44,668 books, approximately 135,000 e-books 
(accessible via login), and more than 5,000 e-journals, along with a rich array 
of digital resources in Science and Technology, Social Sciences, Arts and 
Humanities, Commerce, and Management.
The Library also houses an audio-visual collection of around 200 CDs and 
DVDs. Fully automated through KOHA—an open-source integrated library 
management system, it is seamlessly organized and user-friendly. Subscriptions 
to N-LIST (National Library and Information Services Infrastructure for Scholarly Content) and the Delhi University Library 
System (DULS) further extend access to thousands of e-resources through secure login credentials.
Smart Class Rooms
Maharaja Agrasen College took a significant step toward advancing next-generation 
pedagogy by establishing six state-of-the-art smart classrooms on its campus. Aligned 
with the college’s vision of delivering holistic and high-quality education, these 
classrooms have transformed the teaching-learning environment into a more dynamic 
and inclusive space.
Equipped with real-time e-learning tools—such as audio-visual aids, multimedia 
content, online conferences, Power Point presentations, and 2D and 3D animations, 
these classrooms have enriched both virtual and physical learning experiences. This modern setup seamlessly integrates traditional 
classroom methods with digital platforms, making education more accessible, interactive, and engaging for all students.
‘Sahastradhara’—A Nexus of Thought and Learning
Inaugurated on January 15, 2025, Sahastradhara stands as a vibrant confluence of diverse intellectual streams—a space envisioned 
by the College to foster critical thinking and meaningful dialogue among students. Conceived in alignment with the principles 
of NEP 2020, this informal, open learning environment recognises the transformative potential of experiential education beyond 
traditional classrooms.

CELLS/CENTRES
To enrich this dynamic space, several thoughtfully designed features have been introduced: a herbal garden, yoga and 
meditation parks, vermi and anaerobic composting pits, organic farming beds, seedling plots, a dedicated sowing area, a polyhouse, and a gazebo. Together, these elements cultivate not only ecological awareness but also serve as micro-learning centres 
tailored to the needs of Skill Enhancement Courses (SEC) and Value-Added Courses (VAC), offering immersive, hands-on learning 
opportunities.
Himalayan Study Circle: Exploring the Spirit of the Mountains
Established in August 2022, the Himalayan Study Circle (HSC@MAC) was envisioned as a multi-disciplinary initiative dedicated 
to the exploration of Himalayan ecology and cultural heritage. Affiliated with the Centre for Himalayan Studies, University of 
Delhi, the Study Circle engages in research spanning Himalayan history, geography, society, economy, polity, culture, traditional 
knowledge, bio-resources, environment, and sustainable development. 
Its engagements include:
Fortnightly discussions on diverse themes: geomorphology, cultural landscapes, biodiversity, habitats, and 
 
regional traditions
Documentary screenings featuring globally acclaimed ecological and cultural narratives
Nature walks and field visits to biodiversity parks, national parks, and wildlife sanctuaries
Annual Himalayan Festival, celebrated at both college and university levels
Compilation of an Anthology on Himalayan Culture, capturing the multifaceted essence of the region
Through these activities, HSC@MAC nurtures a spirit of inquiry, appreciation, and preservation, and verbalises the majestic 
Himalayas into the academic and cultural life of the institution.
College Publications
The academic community at Maharaja Agrasen College recognises the importance of fostering in-house publications as a means of 
encouraging scholarly and creative expression. Among its notable initiatives is The Indian Journal of Social Enquiry, a distinguished 
research journal that has secured a prominent place in the landscape of academic publishing across the country.
The Department of Business Economics contributes to this vibrant culture through its annual publication, BEAM, which 
serves as a platform for students to articulate their academic insights and engage with contemporary business trends. Similarly, the 
Department of Journalism regularly brings out its newsletter, MAC VOICE, while the Department of English publishes Edges, a 
creative writing journal dedicated to diverse student expressions. Remarkably, Edges is entirely conceptualised, edited, and designed 
by students, offering them a comprehensive understanding of the journalistic process from content creation to publication.
The college magazine, AGRANIKA, stands as a testament to the collective spirit of the college community. This magazine 
designed by the Magazine and Prospectus Committee of the college provides a vivid canvas for creativity and serves as a chronicle 
of the academic year. Published annually, AGRANIKA is formally released during the college’s Annual Day celebration.
National Service Scheme (NSS)
The National Service Scheme (NSS) stands as a beacon of youth-driven social commitment, devoted to nurturing a sense of civic 
responsibility and service among students. Inspired by the guiding mantra 
 (“May all be happy”) and harmonised

CELLS/CENTRES
with the national ethos of ¶
** and 
, NSS empowers young minds to contribute meaningfully toward 
building an inclusive, equitable society.
Ongoing Projects and Initiatives
Project ABHI (Agrasen Baroji Help Initiative): MAC’s NSS unit has adopted the villages of Baroji (Haryana) 
 
and Nistauli (U.P.) to advance rural development and empowerment. Through consistent field visits, volunteers 
 
educate residents about governance, civic rights, and self-reliance, ensuring the villagers are not merely recipients 
 
of help but function as active change-makers within their communities.
Project Akshar: Focused on holistic education for underprivileged children in Dallupura and Kondli, this initiative 
 
blends academic support with lessons in life skills, hygiene, and confidence-building. Volunteers also host joyful 
 
community events like Diwali Melas and Children’s Day celebrations to nurture emotional and social well-being.
AAROGYA: A public health drive that raises awareness about preventive healthcare and encourages wellnessoriented habits among communities.
Swachhata: Aligned with the Swachh Bharat Abhiyan initiated by the Government of India, this flagship program 
 
promotes environmental cleanliness through regular drives, waste management efforts, and awareness campaigns. 
AGRANI: Focused on empowering women, this initiative addresses gender inequality by creating opportunities 
 
for skill development, expression, and growth.
SYAAHI: A creative platform where volunteers voice social messages through reflective writing to promote peace, 
 
equity, and shared humanity.
SANJAAL: The tech wing of NSS, where volunteers harness digital tools to spread awareness via posters, reels, 
 
and online campaigns. This wing recognises the dynamic role of technology in influencing the masses to lead to a 
 
positive change in society.
TASAVVUR: With artistry at its core, this wing breathes visual life into NSS causes. Volunteers craft impactful 
 
posters, props, and handmade installations to aesthetically enhance outreach efforts.
At its heart, NSS at MAC nurtures the emotion of humanity, weaving compassion and service into every initiative. With 
each thoughtful act, the unit shapes not just better communities, but also conscientious individuals—prepared to lead, uplift, and 
transform the world around them.
The selection to NSS will be undertaken after a round of interview and group discussion. The details will be shared on the 
website in due course of time. 
National Cadet Corps (NCC)
The National Cadet Corps (NCC) is a voluntary 
youth organization under the Indian Armed Forces, 
committed to shaping young individuals into 
disciplined and patriotic citizens. Our college unit, 
MAC-NCC, operates under the 3 Delhi Girls Battalion 
and currently comprises approximately 110 girl cadets.
Despite being a relatively new unit, MAC-NCC has 
demonstrated commendable enthusiasm and initiative. 
Our cadets actively participate in a variety of social and 
environmental initiatives, including cleanliness drives, 
tree plantation campaigns, and the adoption and upkeep of public statues. The unit also organizes impactful events such as lectures, 
awareness talks, and commemorations of International Yoga Day, Environment Day, and other significant occasions.
In recent years, our cadets have proudly represented the unit in several prestigious national-level events and camps, including 
the All India Annual NCC Trek (Darjeeling and Ajmer), Prime Minister’s Rally, Special National Integration Camp, Thal Sainik 
Camp, Independence Day Camp, Youth Exchange Program, Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat Camp, and Mountaineering Training 
Camp, among others.

CELLS/CENTRES
Regular institutional training is conducted on campus by dedicated GCIs and PI staff, ensuring that our cadets receive 
consistent guidance and skill development in alignment with NCC’s core values.
The selection to NCC Girls will be undertaken after a round of interview and presentations. The details will be shared on the 
website in due course of time. 
SC/ST/OBC Cell (As per UGC Guidelines)
The college has established an SC/ST/OBC Cell to support students from these categories. Key functions include:
Addressing student queries through Liaison Officers and committee members
Organising workshops and informative sessions on scholarships and government aid
Hosting motivational lectures to build confidence and readiness
Handling grievances with dedicated staff stationed in the administrative block
Offering an online grievance submission link on the college website
NCWEB Centre – Empowering Girls' Education
The Non-Collegiate Women’s Education Board (NCWEB) provides access to higher education for female residents of the NCT of 
Delhi. The NCWEB Centre at Maharaja Agrasen College has been active since 2011, and currently offers:
B.A. Programme – 355 seats
B.Com. Programme – 230 seats
 
Aspiring candidates are encouraged to visit the official NCWEB website for details on the admission process and course 
 
offerings.
IGNOU
Maharaja Agrasen College serves as a designated study centre for both Postgraduate and Undergraduate programmes offered by 
the Indira Gandhi National Open University (IGNOU). A distinctive feature of the IGNOU curriculum is its Induction Program, 
thoughtfully designed to orient students to the distance learning model.
Weekend classes are conducted for a wide range of programmes, including:
Undergraduate Courses:
 
Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA); Bachelor of Commerce (B.Com); Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.)
Postgraduate Courses:
 
Master of Commerce (M.Com); Master of Business Administration (MBA); Master of Arts in Hindi (MHD); 
 
Master of Arts in Political Science (MPS); Master of Arts in History (MAH); Master of Arts in English (MEG); 
 
Master of Economics (MEC); Master of Public Administration (MPA); Master of Computer Applications (MCA); 
 
Master of Science in Mathematics with Applications in Computer Science (MSCMACS)
Diploma and Certificate Programs:
 
Postgraduate Diploma in Journalism and Mass Communication (PGJMC); Diploma in Teaching German 
 
(DTG); Certificate in German Language; And other various other specialisations
Centre for Performing Arts and Cultural Studies (CPACS)
Since its establishment in 2014, the Centre for Performing Arts and Cultural 
Studies (CPACS) has been at the forefront of promoting theatrical, multimedia, 
and cinematic arts through live demonstrations and immersive engagement. 
An unit of the Department of English, Maharaja Agrasen College, University of 
Delhi, CPACS actively fosters the exploration and appreciation of cultural studies 
and performative practices.
CPACS remained committed to bridging the gap between students and the 
performing arts by disseminating information about online events, workshops,

CELLS/CENTRES
and training opportunities. It offered timely updates on accessible digital platforms and resources, including academic journals 
and virtual events like the MIT Press journal TDR: The Drama Review and student essay competitions—framing performing arts 
as a medium for resilience, healing, and shared care. The Centre continues to collaborate with a diverse range of artists and theatre 
practitioners, fostering a dynamic space for creative exchange.
Sanskriti Kala Kendra
The Department of Hindi at Maharaja Agrasen College proudly established the Sanskriti Kala Kendra with the vision of nurturing 
the holistic development of students. Beyond academic instruction, the Kendra is committed to instilling cultural sensitivity and 
ethical values among learners. Notably, Maharaja Agrasen College has launched a long-term educational and cultural exchange 
programme with Tokyo University of Foreign Studies, Tokyo, Japan—fostering international dialogue and exposure.
To further its objectives, the Centre organises a vibrant array of activities including educational trips, cultural exchange 
programmes, and seminars, symposia, and expert lectures on pressing socio-cultural themes. These initiatives aim to provide 
students with a well-rounded and enriching educational experience.
Civic Education Centre
The Civic Education Centre is dedicated to cultivating constitutional values and responsible citizenship through purposeful 
learning. Central to its mission is the Leadership Development Camp, alongside various civic initiatives across Delhi that encourage 
student leadership and active engagement.
To empower educators, the Centre develops and delivers Teacher Training Programmes in Civic Education, while also working 
on: Designing and implementing monitoring and evaluation mechanisms, Publishing research material, Developing standards and 
curriculum frameworks and Running leadership and network training programmes
Its outreach strategy involves fostering sustained collaboration with key stakeholders, including the Department of Education, 
schools, colleges, homes, and local government officials—to collectively strengthen India’s civic fabric.
Workshops are regularly conducted to instil awareness among students about their rights and duties, and to familiarise 
them with the constitutional provisions that shape democratic life. The Centre also passionately advocates for social justice and 
environmental stewardship, engaging students and the wider community in programmes that support the restoration of natural 
spaces, including parks and rivers.
Gandhi Study Circle
The Gandhi Study Circle is dedicated to exploring the life, ideas, and transformative actions of Mahatma Gandhi. 
Committed to making Gandhian thought relevant to contemporary times, the Study Circle engages participants through a diverse 
array of activities held throughout the year, such as cleanliness drives, reading circles, discussion forums, quizzes, creative writing 
sessions, and debate competitions. 
Women Development Cell
The Women Development Cell is an initiative dedicated to empowering women and fostering gender equality. Every year the 
Cell organises numerous events such as, awareness programs, health camps, and skill-building workshops, to address women's 
issues and promote holistic development. Through collaborations with experts and active student participation, the Cell creates a 
platform for dialogue, learning, and action. Any details pertaining to its activities will be uploaded on the College website. 
Environment Cell: G-ou
The Environment Cell of Maharaja Agrasen College (G-one) is dedicated to fostering environmental awareness and implementing 
proactive solutions to address contemporary ecological challenges. Through its diverse activities, the Cell nurtures an ethically 
conscious and environmentally responsible mindset among all members of the college community.
A significant achievement was realised when the College was declared a Zero Waste Campus, following successful collaborations 
with the Indian Pollution Control Association (IPCA) and Jaagruti Waste Paper Recyclers. These partnerships have enabled 
systematic waste management practices—including the handling of paper, plastic, and e-waste.

CELLS/CENTRES
The Environment Cell undertakes a range of impactful initiatives such as: Plantation 
drives, Interactive workshops and seminars, Panel discussions, Industrial visits 
and Participation in various government projects and sustainability programs
The students from all courses are encouraged to be a part of the Environment 
Cell: G-ou. The details regarding activities and information related to the 
membership of the Environment Cell: G-ou will be uploaded on the college 
website.
Training and Placement Cell (TPC)
In a rapidly globalising economy, securing a strong foothold in the professional world has become increasingly 
competitive. The Training and Placement Cell (TPC) of Maharaja Agrasen College plays a vital role in bridging the gap between 
academic life and professional success by offering students a unified platform for growth and opportunity.
TPC’s mission is to empower students through: 1. Employment and internship opportunities; 2. Development of communication 
and management skills; 3. Cultivation of corporate etiquette and professionalism. To inspire and empower, TPC hosts numerous 
workshops and guest sessions featuring accomplished entrepreneurs and professionals whose journeys encourage students to aim 
higher and dream bigger. It also conducts regular career counselling webinars, helping individuals identify their unique strengths 
and develop the mindset to become standout achievers.
Members of the Cell possess a deep understanding of corporate dynamics and foster a culture of excellence. With strong 
soft skills, articulate communication abilities, and an unwavering commitment to success, they bring passion and innovation to 
everything they do. Their blend of industry insight and youthful creativity makes them invaluable contributors—driving not only 
their personal progress but also enhancing the organisations they engage with.
Equal Opportunity Cell (EOC)
At Maharaja Agrasen College, the Equal Opportunity Cell (EOC) is committed to fostering an inclusive and empathetic campus 
environment. Recognising persons with disabilities as valuable contributors to society, the Cell works tirelessly to promote 
awareness and sensitivity around issues of disability.
EOC plays an instrumental role in: Facilitating counselling services and accessible pedagogy, Introducing assistive technologies 
and Ensuring infrastructural accessibility across campus
To integrate individuals with disabilities into the heart of college life, EOC regularly organises a spectrum of engaging activities 
throughout the year: Disability sensitization workshops, Expert lectures and skill development sessions, Job fairs dedicated to 
inclusivity and Sports and cultural programs.
The Cell’s efforts are rooted in the belief that disability is not a limitation, but a vital aspect of human diversity—deserving of 
dignity, opportunity, and representation in every space.
Abdul Kalam Centre
Named in tribute to India’s visionary leader Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, the Centre is devoted to equipping students with the skills and

CELLS/CENTRES
mindset necessary for future success. It focuses on nurturing competencies that go hand-in-hand with innovative infrastructure, 
preparing learners to thrive in an ever-evolving world.
Institutional Support & Innovation Initiatives
The Centre is dedicated to providing continuous infrastructural and IT assistance alongside brainstorming sessions with faculty 
members and industry professionals to nurture emerging ideas. Select ventures may also receive financial support. In previous 
academic years, the college has successfully completed 10 Innovation Projects and 7 Star Innovation Projects. To enhance academic 
engagement, the college also offers a range of short-term courses and operates an e-learning portal, ensuring a seamless and 
interactive teaching-learning environment.
Short-Term Courses
The college launched short-term courses to enrich the undergraduate curriculum, empower students to face post-graduation 
challenges, and strengthen their soft skills. Courses offered include: Introduction to Data Science for Business using R Programming, 
Data Analysis for Business Decision Making, Sugam Sangeet, Mobile App Designing: Level 1, English Skills for Professional 
Success: Level and Legal Awareness (in collaboration with East Delhi Legal Services Authority)
New short-term courses are slated to begin in the 2025–25 academic session, further supporting students’ skill development and 
knowledge enhancement.
MAC e-Learning Portal
To meet institutional requirements, the college implemented its e-learning platform using the Moodle framework. It 
includes all three-year degree programs and short-term courses, fostering a digitally integrated learning experience.
Embedded Systems and Robotics Centre (ESRC)
The ESRC at MAC serves as a dynamic hub for robotics education, combining theoretical learning with practical application 
of mathematics, computer science, and engineering principles. It strives to deliver impactful training through hands-on 
experimentation, encouraging collaboration between students and educators in the field of embedded systems and robotics.
Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC)
Established on October 3, 2015, in alignment with NAAC guidelines, the MAC-IQAC is committed to enhancing the college’s 
academic and research standards. It aims to build a holistic and consistent quality framework that drives institutional excellence. 
The cell comprises the Principal as Chairperson, department heads, and administrative officials. Together, they foster a culture 
of continuous improvement by institutionalizing best practices and internalizing quality standards across all academic and 
administrative functions.
MAC-EBSB Club
In accordance with the Government of India’s Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat initiative, Maharaja Agrasen College inaugurated the 
MAC-EBSB Club in the 2019–20 academic year. The club comprises of twenty students from diverse disciplines and continues to 
uphold the values of unity in diversity through cultural exchange and collaborative projects.
MAC-EBSB Club: Celebrating Cultural Unity
The departments of Maharaja Agrasen College actively organise events under the guidance of faculty members as part of the 
Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat initiative—a heartfelt endeavour to honour and preserve India’s rich cultural heritage, its history, 
traditions, and values.
From a diverse array of activities, the Club organises 
several events annually. These include: movie screenings. 
article writing and art competitions, a virtual tour to NE 
region of India and a cultural competition under Yuvotsav.
More than just an activity-based group, MAC-EBSB

CELLS/CENTRES
Club offers a vibrant learning platform where students engage with India’s numerous cultural intersections. By fostering meaningful 
insights into diverse traditions and histories, the club plays a vital role in nurturing a holistic and inclusive vision for national 
growth.
The Northeast Cell
The Northeast Cell of Maharaja Agrasen College stands as a tribute to the rich 
cultural tapestry of India’s northeastern states. It provides a dynamic space 
for students from the region to showcase the ethos and heritage of the eight 
northeastern states through thoughtfully curated events and programs.
With a focus on empowering students intellectually and creatively, the 
Cell mentors them into becoming socially responsible and visionary leaders. 
Rooted in the spirit of Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat, it educates peers from 
various parts of India on the unique cultures, traditions, and historical 
legacies of the Northeast.
Over the years, the Northeast Cell has organised numerous impactful 
events, such as Northeast Cultural Fest cum Folk Dance Competition, 
essay, poster making and quiz competitions, etc. Through these initiatives, 
the Cell continues to be a fertile ground for fostering awareness, unity, and 
appreciation of India’s vibrant diversity.
Students’ Union
The Students' Union of Maharaja Agrasen College serves as the highest 
academic representative body for students, dedicated to their welfare 
and development. Functioning in close coordination with the University, 
the Union plays a pivotal role in organising academic and co-curricular 
activities that enrich campus life.
It is a democratically elected body, with selection based on academic 
excellence, regular attendance, sincerity, and demonstrated leadership 
qualities. In collaboration with faculty members of respective departments 
of the college and the Student Advisory Committee, the Union actively 
contributes to the enhancement of college infrastructure and facilities, striving to create a more conducive academic environment. 
Beyond administration, the Students’ Union cultivates teamwork, encourages innovative thinking, and translates collective ideas 
into meaningful action and long-term vision.
Student Advisory Committee
The Student Advisory Committee (SAC) plays a pivotal role in guiding the Students’ Union and providing a broad range of 
opportunities for student participation in academic and co-curricular pursuits. Established in 2013, the Committee began its 
journey with the inauguration of the first National Student Academic Congress on the theme “Educational Reforms: The Way 
Forward.” Since 2016, SAC has also been instrumental in facilitating the democratic election of the College’s Students’ Union, in 
accordance with the University of Delhi’s guidelines and the Lyngdoh Commission’s recommendations. This initiated a new era 
of student leadership and representation at Maharaja Agrasen College, beginning with the election of its first student-led body.
As a vital institutional body, SAC not only supervises the overall functioning of the Students’ Union but also serves as a bridge 
between student representatives and the college administration. Dedicated to the holistic development of students, SAC curates a 
spectrum of enriching experiences—ranging from lectures and talks to workshops and academic congresses—designed to foster 
critical skills, broaden intellectual horizons, and guide students toward career paths aligned with their aspirations and capabilities.

CELLS/CENTRES
Co-Curricular Activities Committee (CCA)
At Maharaja Agrasen College, co-curricular engagement is regarded as a vital component of holistic student development. 
Spearheading these initiatives is the Co-Curricular Activities Committee (formerly the Extra-Curricular Activities Committee), 
comprising dedicated faculty members and a dynamic team of student volunteers.
Students play a pivotal role in enriching the cultural fabric of the college by actively organising and participating in a spectrum 
of events. These are orchestrated through the Cultural Council—a student-led body nominated by the Committee—and an array 
of vibrant cultural societies that operate under its aegis. Each society offers a unique platform for students to cultivate their talents 
and express their creativity.
Under the aegis of CCA, the college proudly hosts a broad range of student societies, including:
Nataraj – Dance Society 
Srijan – Arts and Craft Society

The North-East Society

Vivekananda Study Circle
Adding further distinction, Maharaja Agrasen College also serves as the mentor institution for the SPIC MACAY Delhi and 
NCR East Zone. Among its landmark initiatives is the VIRASAT Series, a week-long celebration that honours India’s classical 
arts and cultural heritage. This festival is thoughtfully curated by the college’s SPIC MACAY Chapter under the guidance of the 
Co-Curricular Activities Committee.
Student Aid Fund & Scholarship Opportunities
Maharaja Agrasen College provides financial assistance to economically disadvantaged students through the Student Aid Fund. 
Eligible students are advised to regularly check the college website for up-to-date information regarding eligibility criteria, 
registration procedures and important deadlines.
Government Scholarship Opportunities may be accessed via National Scholarship Portal – NSP. Eligible students may apply 
for the following schemes: Central Sector Scheme for college and university students, Post Matric Scholarship for Students with 
Disabilities under NSP, SDM Workers Welfare Fund Post-Matric Scholarship, Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for professional and 
technical courses, Post Matric Scholarship – Arunachal Pradesh State Stipend Scheme. 
Additional notable awards include Vice-Chancellor’s Gold Medal for Physically Challenged Students (DU), Sitaram Jindal 
Foundation Scholarship, World Brotherhood Organization Education Scholarship, Shri Sultan Chand Memorial Scholarship, 
Sultan Chand Draupadi Memorial Scholarship, Professor (Dr.) Prem Kumari Srivastava Memorial Award, Late Sh. Vinod Kumar 
Memorial Award, 
 
Apart from the above-mentioned scholarships, students may also apply for Shri Uggersain Memorial Endowment Scholarship, 
All India Entrance Scholarship Exams (DU) Entrance Based, Rector's Prize Exam 2016 (DU) Entrance based & Dr. VK Rao 
Endowment Book Grant.
Additional Scholarships (via e-District Portal) include Post Matric Scholarship for OBC Students (PMS-OBC), Post Matric 
Scholarship for SC Students, Merit Scholarship for SC/ST/OBC/Minority Students (colleges & professional institutions), PMSSS 
Scholarship for students from Jammu & Kashmir, Bihar Student Credit Card Scheme and Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme for 
Central Armed Forces and Assam Rifles.
The complete list of scholarships and awards granted in the previous academic session is mentioned on page 82.

List of Documents Required for Admission
The Candidates shall be required to upload copies of the following certificates/documents at the time of registration and produce 
the documents in original at the time of physical verification at the end of the admissions process. 
 
1. 
Class X Certificate (Mark-sheet or certificate) indicating date of birth and Parents' names & XII Mark-Sheet. 
 
2. 
SC/ST/OBC/EWS/CW/KM Certificate (in the applicant's name) issued by the competent authority. (The names of 
applicants claiming reservation under SC/ST/OBC/EWS/CW/KM must match with the names that appear on their 
corresponding School Board qualifying certificates; similarly, their parents' names must match in both sets of certificates). 
 
3. 
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate (in the name of the applicant) issued by the competent authority, and wherein the 
caste is in the OBC central list issued by http://ncbc.nic.in. (The name of the applicant claiming reservation under OBC 
(Non-Creamy Layer) must match with the applicant's name as it appears on their corresponding School Board qualifying 
certificates; similarly, their parents' names must match in both sets of certificates). As stipulated, an income certificate for 
the financial year 2024-25, dated on or after March 31, 2025, will be required. The format of the OBC certificate is as per 
the DOPT certificate issued in 2014 (Appendix V of the University of Delhi, Bulletin of Information). 
 
4. 
EWS Certificate (Appendix IV of the University of Delhi, Bulletin of Information) from competent authority certifying 
the applicant can claim reservation under this category. (The names of applicants claiming reservation under this category 
must match with the names that appear on their corresponding School Board qualifying certificates; similarly, their 
parents' names must match in both sets of certificates). Income certificate for the financial year 2025-26, dated on or after 
March 31, 2025, as stipulated, is required. 
Admission Procedure
Step I: Appearing in CUET-UG 2025.
 
 
For the academic year 2025-26, admission to all UG Programs of UOD will be done based on the Common University 
Entrance Test- Under-Graduate 2025 (CUET (UG) – 2025. 
 
 
CUET(UG) - 2025 was conducted by the National Testing Agency (NTA) in May-June 2025. 
Step II: Registration in CSAS – 2025 
 
 
Appearing in CUET(UG) - 2025 will not be a sufficient condition to secure a seat in Maharaja Agrasen College. For 
admission to the College UG Programs, a candidate has to apply in Common Seat Allocation System (CSAS-2025) of 
University of Delhi. Determination of program-specific eligibility and verification of documents for admission to MAC 
will be the sole purview of the college/university. 
 
 
All applicants desirous of taking admission to Maharaja Agrasen College must give the preferences of MAC and the 
programs offered by the college in CSAS – 2025. 
 
 
The candidate must refer to the Programs offered by MAC, their Program-Specific Eligibility Criteria, the seat matrix of 
each program offered by the college, fee structure and other relevant information published on the college website and 
the admission website of UOD before selecting the Program and College preferences.
Step II: Registration in CSAS – 2025 
 
 
The CSAS -2025 form will be duly submitted only when the applicant has paid the relevant registration fee. This fee must 
be paid only through the link provided through the applicant's Dashboard.

INFORMATION
Step III: Seat Allocation
 
 
The University of Delhi will provisionally allocate seats to applicants during the various allocation rounds. The applicants 
must ‘Accept’ the allocated seat and apply for admission to MAC. The college will then verify the documents and approve 
the admission. For details, please refer to CSAS - 2025 document available at: https:// admission.uod.ac.in
Step IV: Selection of Course and College
Upon seat allocation, applicants must log in to their Dashboards to accept the program and college allocated. 
 
i. 
Please note that ‘Accept’ option to apply in the college as per allocated seat can be made only online through their 
 
 
Dashboard on the UG admissions portal. There will be no physical visit to the colleges at this point in the process. 
 
ii. The procedure for accepting the allocated seat by the applicant must be completed within the stipulated time period. 
Step V: Online verification of documents by respective colleges
The college shall verify the documents uploaded by the applicant for the program-specific eligibility, minimum eligibility and 
category. Those approved are required to pay the fee and receive a confirmation of their admission. 
Step VI: Payment of fees to confirm admission
Once the Principal of the College has approved their admission, the applicants will receive a link on their Dashboard through 
which they must submit the program fees due. This fee can only be paid online through the portal. 
i. The applicant must pay the fee without delay within the stipulated time. On successful payment of fees, the applicant is granted 
provisional admission to the said college. 
ii. Applicants must pay the fee within the timeframe allocated, failing which it will be concluded that the applicant is not interested 
in the program of study at that college, and the candidate will be out of CSAS - 2025. 
Step VII: Physical verification of the original documents
The respective colleges will verify the uploaded documents within the stipulated time (Dates for the same shall be announced by 
the University / College later). If, at this stage, it is found that the information given by the applicant is false and/or is not supported 
by the documents presented, the admission will be immediately cancelled. No fees will be refunded in such cases. 
Any change in the admission process for CSAS – 2025 UG admission will be notified on the website of University of Delhi. 
Candidates applying to the University of Delhi must refer to the UG Bo I-2025 for Common Minimum Eligibility criteria and 
Program-Specific Eligibility criteria. 
If at any stage original documents relating to the admission of a candidate are found to be fake / non-genuine or fabricated or 
in any other manner defective, the said candidate will not be admitted. If already admitted, admission will be cancelled without 
any prior notice in this regard. If the same is found after completing the program, his / her degree will be cancelled and appropriate 
legal action will be taken. 
Qualifying Examinations:
Qualifying examinations for admission to the first year of undergraduate programs offered by the University of Delhi shall be 
Senior Secondary School Certificate Examination (Class XII) of the Central Board of Secondary Education or an examination 
recognized as equivalent 
Age Requirement:
As per Ordinance-I of the University of Delhi, there is no minimum age bar for admission for the courses offered by the college.
